core_mechanism: a local-coupling rule on a collection of interacting units in which each unit couples to a fixed number k of its nearest neighbours (topological / rank-based connectivity) rather than to every unit within a fixed metric radius, making the interaction network invariant to the density of the medium and thereby preserving global cohesion under density perturbations
brief_reason: The abstract establishes that each bird interacts on average with a fixed number of neighbours (six-seven) rather than with all neighbours inside a fixed metric distance, and argues this fixed-count topological coupling keeps the aggregation cohesive against large density changes.
